message:
fix(core): replace saturating arithmetic with checked overflow + preflight validation

Replace all saturating_add/saturating_sub on inode.blocks, links_count,
and btrfs nlink with checked arithmetic that returns FfsError::Corruption
on overflow or underflow. This prevents silent counter corruption from
propagating through the filesystem.

Key changes:

- ext4_checked_inode_blocks_delta(): checked u64 block counter arithmetic
  with i128 delta, used across write, fallocate, truncate, orphan
  recovery, e2compr cluster writes, xattr block allocation, and
  punch_hole paths

- ext4_checked_links_count_delta(): checked u16 link counter arithmetic
  for mkdir, unlink, rmdir, rename, and hard-link operations

- btrfs_checked_nlink_delta() + btrfs_validate_nlink_adjustment():
  preflight nlink bounds checks before any mutating btrfs tree operations

- Preflight validation for directory entry insertion on both ext4
  (ext4_preflight_dir_entry_insert) and btrfs
  (btrfs_preflight_dir_entry_insert, btrfs_preflight_inode_ref_insert)

- validate_single_path_component(): reject names containing '/' or
  exceeding u8::MAX length before touching any on-disk state

- btrfs_require_directory_inode(): guard against operating on
  non-directory inodes in mkdir, create, unlink, rename, link, symlink

- Rollback on ext4 create/mkdir/symlink/link when ext4_add_dir_entry
  fails: delete_inode or restore original inode to avoid leaked
  allocations

- Ext4XattrPostInodeAction enum + apply_ext4_xattr_post_inode_actions():
  defer xattr block mutations until after inode write succeeds, with
  rollback of allocated blocks on inode write failure

- BtrfsPurgeAction type alias + btrfs_collect_purge_plan /
  btrfs_validate_purgeable_items / btrfs_execute_purge_plan: validate
  all items are parseable before committing to purge

- btrfs_remove_dir_entry split into btrfs_remove_named_dir_item and
  btrfs_remove_named_dir_index for finer-grained error isolation

- btrfs_remove_inode_ref now returns Corruption instead of silently
  succeeding when the INODE_REF key is missing

- BtrfsMutationError::KeyNotFound matched explicitly in all
  update-or-insert patterns instead of catching all errors

- btrfs setxattr/removexattr now touch ctime on the inode

- ext4 rename preflight validates the target before any mutations,
  pre-computes the '..' directory block update for cross-parent renames

- Comprehensive new tests: create/mkdir with invalid names roll back
  inode allocation, write with inode.blocks at u64::MAX returns
  Corruption without leaking blocks, mkdir with parent nlink at u16::MAX
  returns Corruption without leaking inodes or blocks, rename over
  directory with corrupt parent nlink preserves both names, rename with
  invalid new name preserves source

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
